description: Synthetic ID: RC- prefix + source award ID
current_end_date if potential_end_date is null, else potential_end_date
Which date was used: current_end_date / potential_end_date / unknown / terminated (a complete_likely termination retargeted the expiration to the termination date)
FACT — some transaction carried a termination reason-for-modification code (action_type_code E/F/X/N; K=Close Out is never a termination)
FACT — earliest termination code (E=default, F=convenience, X=cause, N=legal cancellation); empty when not terminated
FACT — earliest termination transaction's action date; empty when not terminated

ESTIMATE (inferred, conservative) — complete_likely only when the reported current end collapsed to within 31 days of the termination date; else partial_or_unclear; none when not terminated. DoD FPDS reporting lags ~90 days.
Basis for the termination read: observed_code / none
FACT — distinct FPDS transactions observed for the award in the loaded fiscal-year window
ESTIMATE — mod_count per active PoP-year; empty when history is unreadable (single transaction / unusable dates)
ESTIMATE — low / normal / high (fitted priors in config/mods_signal.yaml) / not_applicable
Derived from facts — last/first positive CUMULATIVE ceiling (potential_total_value_of_award) across the award's transactions; empty when <2 positive readings
ESTIMATE — ceiling_growth_ratio above the fitted balloon threshold (1.5)
Basis for the ceiling read: measured / insufficient
ESTIMATE (weak) — a non-closeout transaction below the deobligation floor before the planned end; never labeled a cancellation
ESTIMATE — a non-competed (extent B/C/G) transaction pushed the current end >30 days past the ORIGINAL planned end (FPDS codes only, never text)
Basis for the bridge read: observed / insufficient
History-coverage label for the count/velocity/ceiling reads: measured / single_transaction / insufficient

ESTIMATE (inferred) — a later award in the same NAICS x PSC-class x DoD-component cell shows FPDS activity after this end date (same-parent-IDV task orders and the award itself excluded); empty = Unknown (thin cell)
Basis: observed / none_visible (no successor visible in public data yet — DoD reporting lags ~90 days; NEVER a 'missed recompete') / insufficient_cell

ESTIMATE — how many of the readable displacement signals fired (bridge / termination / deobligation / lapsed_no_successor / sole_offer / size_shift); empty = Unknown (insufficient coverage). A categorical lane — NEVER blended into pursuit_score or any tier.
Coverage fact — how many of the 6 displacement inputs were readable on this record
ESTIMATE — '+'-joined slugs of the fired displacement signals ('none' when none fired); empty = Unknown
Coverage fact — '+'-joined slugs of the displacement inputs that could NOT be read ('none' when all 6 read)
ESTIMATE — multiple_signals / single_signal / none_observed / not_applicable (insufficient coverage)
Basis for the displacement lane: observed / insufficient (fewer than min_signals_read readable inputs — Unknown, never guessed)

ESTIMATE (directional, per-procurement) — the vendor's same-NAICS awards' CO size determination moved S (small) to O (other-than-small) over time; empty = Unknown (insufficient determinations). Never a vendor-size verdict.
Named basis for size_standard_shift (counts + codes per the flagged NAICS cell, or the insufficient label)

Derived from facts — the top incumbent's share of the component's attributed (UEI-known) expiring obligated dollars within the REPORTABLE (Data-Gap-excluded) set — the denominator excludes dollars with no incumbent UEI (bounded by the coverage gate); a ratio of two sums of published obligated-dollar facts (like hhi_concentration); empty = Unknown (see concentration_basis). NOT market share, market power, or contestability.
Coverage fact — distinct positive-net incumbent UEIs in the component's reportable set (always published, even when the share is Unknown)
Basis for the concentration read: observed / insufficient (thin market, low UEI coverage, no positive dollars, or no reportable rows — Unknown, never guessed)
Named refusal reason when concentration_basis is insufficient; empty when observed
FACT — unique per emitted transaction ({award_id}:{modification_number}, deterministically suffixed on collision)
FACT — FPDS reason-for-modification code of the signal-bearing transaction (E/F/X/N terminations, K closeouts, etc.)
FACT — the transaction's obligation delta (federal_action_obligation)
FACT — transaction free text (bounded); LOCAL export only — excluded from every public artifact (sample + Release) and PII-scrubbed locally
ESTIMATE — not an official government date. See scoring_methodology.md
ESTIMATE — not an official government date. See scoring_methodology.md
ESTIMATE — weighted composite of 8 component scores, 0-100
ESTIMATE — derived from pursuit_score thresholds
NAICS code as reported by USAspending
Product/Service Code as reported by USAspending
ESTIMATE — High/Medium/Low/Not Classified rules-based classification

ESTIMATE — value-weighted share of the vendor's forward book (days_until_expiration >= 0) expiring within 180 days; empty = Unknown (see vulnerability_basis). Read next to number_of_cyber_it_awards (n). See scoring_methodology.md
Basis for incumbent_vulnerability_score: value_weighted_near_term_share (score present) / no_forward_book / insufficient_expiration_coverage (score empty — Unknown, never an imputed 0)
Share (%) of the vendor's total obligated value on already-expired rows (days_until_expiration < 0) — excluded from the vulnerability denominator
Share (%) of the vendor's total obligated value with no usable expiration date — excluded from the vulnerability denominator
SYNTHETIC — the pursuing vendor profile used by scoring.py is entirely fictional (see config.py). It is never exported to any Tableau CSV; it only informs pursuit_score/priority_tier calculations.
ESTIMATE — fuzzy-match confidence; 'No Match' for all rows until SAM.gov data is pulled locally

Descriptive incumbent obligated-dollar concentration (top-incumbent dollar-share + incumbent count) of the expiring reportable set (Data Gap excluded), computed live in scoring.market_concentration.compute_hhi_concentration. A ratio of two sums of published obligated-dollar facts — a fact, not an estimate. Markets below min_market_ueis distinct incumbents or the UEI-coverage floor render 'Unknown' — never an imputed number. NOT a market-power, market-share, or contestability claim. The SAME read is baked per-component onto dim_agency as the concentration_* columns (annotate_agency_concentration) for the capture brief's Office section. See scoring_methodology.md
